title: PER_PACKAGE shipping methods with 2+ packages get their correctly-priced rate filtered out, dropping cost to null
description:
> Foreman-ready Linear ticket. Label `agent:tars`, assigned to me, state Todo.
> Promote via Linear MCP once reviewed.

## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: shipping-service
```

## Summary

`ShippingRateService.ts` (~line 344) re-quantizes the *combined* package list to build the weight-band filter for every shipping method, including `PER_PACKAGE` ones. For a `PER_PACKAGE` method the rate was built by quantizing **each package separately** and summing the results (`CarrierRateService.getCachedCarrierRatesPerPackage`), so the synthesized rate is stamped at the *summed-brackets* weight. The filter's aggregate re-quantization of the same packages almost never lands on that same value once there are 2+ packages, so `filterRatesByBand` (`ShippingMethod.ts:98`) rejects a rate that was, moments earlier in the same request, correctly priced. Fix the filter so `PER_PACKAGE` methods are matched against the same per-package-summed weight the rate was built at, not a fresh aggregate quantization.

## Context

Diagnosed live against production `shipping-service` via Datadog log trace (request `27b5cc4c-81d0-4491-b629-256e7f735780`, shipment `al9ecvzhhv0jpvi7vgj0t4an` / `WS7191468`, carrier GOPEOPLE / service GP-EXP, shipping method `ybnxpur7ttejqqscjbv2ddcb`, 2 packages @ 4kg each):

1. `CarrierRateService.getCachedCarrierRatesPerPackage` (`projects/shipping-service/src/modules/shipping/appServices/CarrierRateService.ts:360-421`) splits the shipment's packages into one cache lookup per package (`query.packages.map(pkg => ...packages: [pkg])`), quantizes each individually (4kg → bracket 4kg for both), finds a matching `CarrierRate` per package, and sums cost + weight across them: `weightKg += ...` → total `8kg`, synthesized into a `CarrierRate`/`ShippingRate` with `weightMin === weightMax === 8`. Log: `Return the CarrierRate for ShippingMethod ybnxpur7ttejqqscjbv2ddcb (per package)`, then `Synthesize ShippingRates for 1 CarrierRates for ShippingMethod ybnxpur7ttejqqscjbv2ddcb` with `costPrice=8.32 ... sellPrice=11.99` — **a valid rate was computed.**
2. `ShippingRateService.ts:344` then builds the post-hoc weight-band filter: `shippingMethod.getQuantizeWeight(requestPackages)` called with **all packages combined** (not per-package). `calculateDeadWeight` sums to `8kg`; GoPeople's bracket ladder for this carrier account is `[0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 15, 20]` — there's no `8` bracket, so quantizing `8kg` as one aggregate parcel rounds up to bracket **10**.
3. `filterRatesByBand` (`ShippingMethod.ts:98`, single-point band case): `billableWeightValue(10) === rate.weightMin(8)` → **false**. The rate built in step 1 is discarded.
4. `ShipmentService` logs `No quoted shipping rate found for the shipment's shipping method ybnxpur7ttejqqscjbv2ddcb` and sets `cost`/`currency` to `null` on the shipment — despite a correct rate having existed for the same request a few hundred milliseconds earlier.

This reproduces identically on a second, independent shipment (`d89lpit29voo1hvhn9pb2oz0` / `WS7181603`, same carrier service, 2×2.52kg packages to a different destination). Both were manually retried against prod (platform "recalculate cost" action) during this investigation and both failed with this exact trace, confirming it is a live, current defect — not a stale/pre-cache artifact. It will hit **any** `PER_PACKAGE` shipping method with 2+ packages whenever `Σ(quantize(packageᵢ))` doesn't equal `quantize(Σ packageᵢ)`, which is true almost anytime you have 2+ non-trivial packages (per-package rounding compounds upward faster than a single combined rounding does).

DB scope check: of 2,620 GoPeople `SHIPPED`/`QUOTE` shipments updated since 2026-07-08 (after PR #650, an unrelated already-merged/deployed case-insensitivity fix for `TO_LOCALITY` city matching — a *different* bug, do not conflate), 2,597 (99.1%) have a cost populated. The 23 stragglers are consistent with being the multi-package `PER_PACKAGE` subset hitting this exact bug — i.e. this is narrow in blast radius but 100% reproducible within that subset.

**Related prior art — do not duplicate, reference instead:** [ENG-5301](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5301/carrier-rate-cache-quantizer-uses-aggregate-billable-weight-may) ("Carrier rate cache: quantizer uses aggregate billable weight, may diverge from per-article carrier pricing") was split from [ENG-5300](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5300/carrier-rate-cache-cubicallowance-defaults-to-60-in-seed-mis) and **canceled** 2026-06-01 without a fix landing. It covers a related-but-distinct concern: whether the **cache-write/read** quantization should be per-article because some carriers (inferred, unconfirmed for AusPost) may *bill* per-article. That ticket was gated on confirming carrier billing basis first and never proceeded.

This ticket is narrower and does **not** require resolving that open question: `PER_PACKAGE` shipping methods (GoPeople included) **already** quantize and cache per-package correctly on the read side (`getCachedCarrierRatesPerPackage` — this is the code [ENG-5301](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5301/carrier-rate-cache-quantizer-uses-aggregate-billable-weight-may) was asking for, and it exists and works). The bug here is purely that a *second*, independent quantization call in the **downstream filter** (`ShippingRateService.ts:344`) doesn't know about that per-package convention and re-aggregates, discarding a rate that was already correctly computed. Fixing this ticket does not require reopening or resolving [ENG-5301](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5301/carrier-rate-cache-quantizer-uses-aggregate-billable-weight-may)'s carrier-billing-basis question. Also unrelated to [ENG-5474](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5474/quantize-the-shippingrate-read-filter-weight-per-consignment-remove)/[ENG-5485](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5485/eng-5474-redo-fix-warehouse-ratebracket-seed-for-quantized-per) (same filter file, but those address the `PER_CONSIGNMENT` path's cubic double-calculation — a different rate-calculation method, different root cause).

## Out of scope

* Do not touch `PER_CONSIGNMENT` filtering logic or the [ENG-5474](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5474/quantize-the-shippingrate-read-filter-weight-per-consignment-remove)/5485 cubic-double-calc work.
* Do not resolve or reopen [ENG-5301](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5301/carrier-rate-cache-quantizer-uses-aggregate-billable-weight-may)'s "does AusPost bill per-article" question — orthogonal.
* Do not change `CarrierRateService.getCachedCarrierRatesPerPackage`'s per-package summing behavior — it is correct and should stay the source of truth for what weight a `PER_PACKAGE` rate was built at.
* No changes to `CarrierWeightBracket.quantizeWeight` bracket-resolution semantics.
* No backfill/data migration for the 23 currently-affected shipments — file that as a follow-up ops task once the fix is live, not part of this ticket.

## Instructions

### 1. Reproduce with a failing test

In `projects/shipping-service/src/modules/shipping/appServices/__test__/ShippingRateService.test.ts`, add a case: a `PER_PACKAGE` shipping method with a bracket ladder that has no bracket exactly at "sum of two per-package brackets" (mirror the prod case: brackets `[..., 4, 5, 7, 10, ...]`, two packages that each quantize to bracket `4`, summed weight `8`). Assert that a cached `ShippingRate` stamped at `weightMin === weightMax === 8` for that shipping method **survives** the weight-band filter and is returned. Confirm it currently fails (filtered out).

### 2. Fix the filter in `ShippingRateService.ts` (~line 338-355)

For shipping methods where `rateCalculationMethod === ShippingMethodRateCalculationMethod.PER_PACKAGE`, do not call `shippingMethod.getQuantizeWeight(requestPackages)` on the combined package array. Instead, either:

* (a) skip the weight-band re-filter entirely for `PER_PACKAGE` methods when the candidate rate(s) came from the per-package path — mirroring how quantize-failure already returns `subset` unfiltered a few lines above (`quantizeResult.isFailure` branch) — the rate was already correctly resolved at read time and re-filtering by a different quantization basis is the bug, not a safety net; or
* (b) compute `filterWeight` for `PER_PACKAGE` methods as `Σ getQuantizeWeight([pkg]).bracket` per package (mirroring `getCachedCarrierRatesPerPackage`'s own per-package loop) instead of `getQuantizeWeight(requestPackages)` on the full array.

Prefer (a) unless the codebase already has a clean seam for (b) — check whether `ShippingMethod` exposes a public per-package quantize helper before adding one. Keep `PER_CONSIGNMENT` behavior on this line completely unchanged.

### 3. Verify

```bash
cd ~/invenco/foreman/<worktree>
yarn nx test shipping-service --testFile=ShippingRateService.test.ts
yarn nx test shipping-service --testFile=CarrierRateService.test.ts
yarn nx typecheck shipping-service
yarn nx lint shipping-service
```

Confirm the new regression test passes and no existing `PER_CONSIGNMENT` filter tests regress.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New regression test: a `PER_PACKAGE` shipping method with 2+ packages, whose summed per-package quantized weight doesn't match the aggregate quantization of the combined packages, still returns its cached rate through the filter
- [ ] Existing `PER_CONSIGNMENT` and single-package `PER_PACKAGE` filter tests unchanged and passing
- [ ] Typecheck + lint pass
- [ ] No unrelated diff churn (do not touch `PER_CONSIGNMENT` code paths or [ENG-5301](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5301/carrier-rate-cache-quantizer-uses-aggregate-billable-weight-may)'s cache-read quantization)
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Verification gate (post-merge)

- [ ] PR merged to `master`
- [ ] CircleCI deployment verified for merge commit: `shipping-service` prod deploy stage (`Deploy prod` / `PROD deploy compute` / `PROD deploy assets`) succeeded
- [ ] If `shipping-service` triggers E2E: E2E run for the merge commit passed (after reconciliation against pre-existing failures)
- [ ] Spot-check in prod: re-trigger the platform "recalculate cost" action on shipment `al9ecvzhhv0jpvi7vgj0t4an` (`WS7191468`) or `d89lpit29voo1hvhn9pb2oz0` (`WS7181603`) and confirm `cost`/`currency` populate instead of null (via Datadog log trace, same as this ticket's diagnosis)

> See the `circleci-verification` skill for REST-API recipes.

## Relevant Learnings

* Learnings search command (`foreman learnings search ...`) was not available in this environment — not run. Check manually before starting: `foreman learnings search automation-pilot --repo shared --repo shipping-service --query "PER_PACKAGE weight bracket quantize filter"`.

## After Foreman picks this up

* Linear state will flip Todo → In Progress (Foreman owns lifecycle while running)
* Branch: `shipping-service/<linear-default>`

title: Push injection: retrieve top-k relevant learnings at attempt-render time into execution/review/retry prompts
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
```

## Summary

At attempt-render time, embed the selected task's context (title + description), retrieve
the top-k relevant learnings for the repo scope via the existing hybrid pipeline, and
render a compact **Relevant Learnings** digest — id + title + the learning's `**Rule:**`
line — into the execution, retry, review, and review-continuation prompts. Hard caps:
k = 5, ≤600 tokens for the whole section, relevance floor (see DI2 in the batch PLAN).
Agents fetch full bodies with `foreman learnings get` as they already do. No migration.

## Context

Retrieval telemetry (M0) showed ~90% of attempts never read a learning; hybrid search
(M2b) fixed retrieval quality (recall@5 0.676 vs FTS 0.474, committed in
`src/eval/retrieval/README.md`) but is still opt-in — the agent must think to search.
This ticket flips it to default-on, which is the plan's biggest behavioral lever (§R4).
The digest format (titles + Rule lines only, ~100 tokens each) keeps the injection cheap
and non-crowding; the `markApplied` discipline line already lives in
`prompts/fragments/learning-policy.md` and starts carrying real signal once learnings
arrive by default.

## Out of scope

* Injection telemetry / hit-rate (ticket-2 — depends on this one).
* Reviewer, planning, consolidation, deployment prompts (deliberate — DI3).
* Any change to hybrid search internals, thresholds, or the bench fixtures.
* Promotion/decay/lifecycle (M5).

## Instructions

### 1. Retrieval seam

New module `src/execution/inject-relevant-learnings.ts` (intent-named — no utils files):
given `{ task, repoKey, learnings: LearningRepo, embedder: Embedder, warn }`, embed
`task.title + "\n" + task.description` (one query), retrieve over scope
`[repoKey, "shared"]` through the hybrid path, apply the relevance floor (DI2) and the
k = 5 / ≤600-token caps, and return the rendered section (or `null` for "inject nothing").

* Hybrid only (DI1): if the pipeline would fall back to FTS — coverage shortfall, embedder
  init/inference failure, empty corpus — return `null` with one warning log. Reuse the
  gating in `src/retrieval/hybrid-learning-search.ts` rather than duplicating it, but pass
  retrieval options that do NOT increment `read_count` (injection is not a read — the
  ~10% → >80% read metric must stay honest). If arm provenance is needed for DI2, extend
  the fused search to report which arm proposed each result; do not change ranking.
* Extract the digest line from the learning content's `**Rule:**` line (the learning-policy
  fragment mandates it); fall back to the title alone when absent. One entry ≈
  `- <id> — <title>` + the Rule line.
* Token cap: estimate at 4 chars/token; drop lowest-ranked entries until the section fits.

### 2. Render integration

`src/execution/render-worker-prompt.ts`: add a `relevant-learnings` context key rendered
via the new module, and add `{{context:relevant-learnings}}` to
`prompts/templates/execution.md`, `retry.md`, `review.md`, `review-continuation.md`.
The section must tell the agent: fetch full bodies with
`foreman learnings get {{workspace:name}} --id <id> ... --caller {{session:action}}`
before relying on one, and mark ones that materially influenced the work with an `update`
mutation carrying `markApplied: true` (reinforcing, not duplicating, learning-policy).

* `renderWorkerPrompt` gains an optional injection dep (embedder + learnings repo are
  already reachable from `attempt-executor` — thread them through, keep them optional).
  When absent, render exactly as today: the eval harness (`src/eval/run.ts`) and any
  caller that does not opt in must be byte-identical to the pre-ticket output.
* A `null` digest renders a short placeholder or omits the section — pick one and assert
  it in tests; do not leave a dangling template slot.

### 3. Failure discipline

Injection can never fail a render: any error in embed/retrieve/render of the digest is
caught at the seam, logged with the same loudness as retrieval-telemetry failures, and
the prompt renders without the section. A degraded prompt must not become a failed
attempt.

### 4. Verify

`pnpm run check` + `pnpm test`. Unit tests with `FakeEmbedder` + seeded repo: digest
renders top-k with ids and Rule lines; token cap drops the tail; FTS-fallback and
embedder-failure paths inject nothing and do not throw; `read_count` unchanged by
injection; render without the injection dep is byte-identical to before. Prompt-render
tests assert structured fields, not one narrative string (learning
01KT0ZJE4X59QR63CBW27JTRNM). Manually render one real attempt prompt against the live
workspace snapshot and paste the section into the PR description.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Execution/retry/review/review-continuation prompts carry a Relevant Learnings digest (id + title + Rule line) when hybrid retrieval is available
- [ ] k ≤ 5, section ≤600 tokens, relevance floor applied (DI2 lean or a bench-validated alternative, documented in the PR)
- [ ] FTS-fallback / embedder-failure / empty-corpus paths inject nothing, warn once, and never fail the render (test-covered)
- [ ] Injection does not increment `read_count` (test-covered)
- [ ] Rendering without the injection dep is unchanged — eval harness output byte-identical (test-covered)
- [ ] No migration; no changes to hybrid ranking or bench fixtures
- [ ] `pnpm run check` + `pnpm test` pass; no unrelated diff churn
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Verification gate (post-merge)

- [ ] PR merged to `master`
- [ ] Repo `foreman`: **skip** CircleCI deployment verification (no deployment)

## Relevant Learnings

* 01KT0ZJE4X59QR63CBW27JTRNM: Assert structured payload fields in prompt-render tests, not just one narrative field
* 01KX2D1BY9QP576NZXET0A16X1: Guard an async-derived write on the source text it was derived from, in one statement

## After Foreman picks this up

* Branch: auto-derived from the Linear issue
* PR auto-detected via `branchName`; ticket transitions to In Review on PR open

title: Injection telemetry: per-attempt injection events joined against markApplied → injected→applied hit-rate
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Depends on tasks: ENG-5685
```

## Summary

Record which learnings were injected into which attempt's prompt (new table
`learning_injection_event`, migration `0032_learning_injection_event.sql` — number
pre-assigned, do not renumber), stamp the ones the attempt later marked applied, and
surface the two M4 exit metrics behind a CLI command: **injected→applied hit-rate** and
**% of attempts with ≥1 learning injected**. This makes `applied_count` a measured
outcome of injection rather than an honor-system counter, and is the signal M5's
promotion rules will consume.

## Context

Plan item 10 (§4 M4): the injection ticket creates the behavioral lever; this ticket makes
its effect measurable. The join is possible because both halves happen inside one
attempt: `renderWorkerPrompt` knows what it injected, and `worker-result-applier`
processes the same attempt's `learningMutations` — an `update` with `markApplied: true`
already increments `applied_count`
(`src/repos/impl/sqlite-learning-repo.ts` ~line 250). Existing telemetry
(`learning_search_event`) has no attempt dimension, which is why injection gets its own
table rather than another nullable column.

## Out of scope

* Any change to what gets injected (ranking, floor, caps — ticket-1 owns those).
* UI surfacing of injection stats (follow-up once numbers exist; CLI is the surface here).
* Promotion/decay rules consuming the hit-rate (M5).
* Backfilling historical attempts (no injection existed before ticket-1).

## Instructions

### 1. Migration `0032_learning_injection_event.sql`

One row per injected learning per attempt: `id` (ULID PK), `attempt_id`, `task_id`,
`action` (execution/retry/review), `learning_id TEXT REFERENCES learning(id) ON DELETE CASCADE` — **state the ON DELETE action explicitly at creation** (learning
01KX2CSQS5GJ7Y2PS5ZJ8RQ6KA; CASCADE because a deleted learning's injection history is
telemetry about a row that no longer exists), `rank`, `score`, `applied_at TEXT NULL`,
`created_at`. Index on `attempt_id` and `learning_id`.

### 2. Record at the injection seam

Where ticket-1's `inject-relevant-learnings` returns a non-null digest, persist one event
row per injected learning (sync repo method, vectors/ids passed in — same sync-repo
discipline as everywhere). Telemetry-never-fails: a failed insert warns and the render
proceeds.

### 3. Stamp applied at result-apply time

`src/orchestration/worker-result-applier.ts`: when applying an `update` mutation with
`markApplied: true`, also stamp `applied_at` on that attempt's injection rows for that
learning id (attempt id is in scope — `input.attempt.id`). A markApplied for a learning
that was NOT injected is fine and stamps nothing here — the hit-rate numerator is
injected∧applied by construction.

### 4. Surface the metrics

`foreman learnings injection-stats <workspace> [--since <iso>]`: total attempts with ≥1
injection, injected→applied hit-rate (distinct injected learnings applied by the same
attempt ÷ distinct injected), and the top applied learning ids. Report structured fields
(and `--json`), not one narrative string.

### 5. Verify

`pnpm run check` + `pnpm test`. Integration test on a seeded workspace DB: render with
injection → apply a worker result carrying `markApplied: true` for an injected id →
stats show the join; a markApplied for a non-injected id does not inflate the numerator;
learning deletion cascades its events. Run the migration against a copy of the live
snapshot and paste `injection-stats` output into the PR description.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration `0032_learning_injection_event.sql` with explicit ON DELETE, applied cleanly to a live-snapshot copy
- [ ] Every non-null injection persists one event row per learning; failures degrade with a warning, never fail the render (test-covered)
- [ ] `markApplied: true` in the same attempt stamps `applied_at` on the matching injection rows (test-covered, including the non-injected-learning case)
- [ ] `foreman learnings injection-stats` reports % attempts with ≥1 injection + injected→applied hit-rate, human and `--json` output
- [ ] `pnpm run check` + `pnpm test` pass; no unrelated diff churn
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Verification gate (post-merge)

- [ ] PR merged to `master`
- [ ] Repo `foreman`: **skip** CircleCI deployment verification (no deployment)

## Relevant Learnings

* 01KX2CSQS5GJ7Y2PS5ZJ8RQ6KA: Choose a SQLite foreign key's ON DELETE action in the migration that creates it
* 01KX2E8P127XJEZP9K8MFG6AHT: Key a freshness predicate to what invalidates the derived row, not to the source row's updated_at
* 01KT0ZJE4X59QR63CBW27JTRNM: Assert structured payload fields in prompt-render tests, not just one narrative field

## After Foreman picks this up

* Linear state will flip Todo → In Progress once ticket-1 is terminal (Foreman gates on `Depends on tasks`)
* Branch: auto-derived from the Linear issue
* PR auto-detected via `branchName`; ticket transitions to In Review on PR open

title: Fix two tests left failing on master by the ENG-5635 / ENG-5636 merge seam
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
```

## Summary

Two tests fail on `master`. They were introduced on opposite sides of the ENG-5635 / ENG-5636 seam and only break once both are merged, so neither PR could have caught them alone. Both were flagged in review on PR #125, but it merged (and `eng-5635` was deleted) before they could be addressed; the stack then went to `master` via PR #124.

Reproduced against files identical to `origin/master`:

```
× 0030_learning_embedding_text_snapshot > re-embedding an adopted row repairs it, so the corpus converges
    SqliteError: no such column: duplicate_of
× persistence repos > skips a non-finite candidate instead of letting NaN poison the nearest-neighbour scan
    ForemanError: embedding_vector_not_finite
```

## Instructions

### 1. `src/repos/__tests__/learning-embedding-snapshot-migration.test.ts`

`openPreSnapshotDatabase()` builds a partial schema — `readdirSync(...).filter((entry) => entry.endsWith(".sql") && entry < SNAPSHOT_MIGRATION)` — so it stops below `0030`, and the test then applies `0030` alone. Migration `0031_learning_duplicate_flag_provenance.sql` (ENG-5636) never runs, but it adds `duplicate_of` / `source_task_id` to `learning` and `LEARNING_COLUMNS` now selects them. Any repo read through `getLearningsByIds` therefore throws.

Apply the migrations **above** `0030` after `0030` has run, so the pre-0030 seed still exercises 0030's backfill (the point of the test) while the repo sees the schema it expects.

Note `BENCH_MIGRATIONS` in `src/eval/retrieval/run.ts` already had `0031` added for exactly this reason. This is the second place that builds a partial schema and it was missed — worth checking whether a shared helper is warranted so the next migration does not have to find both.

### 2. `src/repos/__tests__/db.test.ts` (~line 1309)

`skips a non-finite candidate instead of letting NaN poison the nearest-neighbour scan` seeds a NaN vector through `upsertLearningEmbedding`, which since ENG-5635 asserts the vector is rankable — the setup throws before the scan is ever reached.

The scenario is also now unreachable by construction: `getCurrentLearningEmbeddings` filters unrankable vectors, so a NaN candidate never enters `nearestLearningEmbedding`'s loop. Plant the row with raw SQL the way the rot tests do (rot at rest is the only way one gets there now) and assert the scan still returns `valid` — that pins the filter that actually protects it.

The wrong-width sibling test is unaffected: a stored vector narrower than the query is self-consistent, passes both write guards, and the skip inside the loop is what catches it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm test` shows neither failure; both tests assert the behaviour they were written for, rather than being deleted or relaxed
- [ ] `pnpm run check` passes
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

title: Embedding substrate: local embedder port, learning_embedding side table, backfill, write-path embed
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
```

## Summary

Add an embedder port (`src/embeddings/embedder.ts`) with one local in-process provider
(fastembed, `bge-small-en-v1.5`, 384 dims), a `learning_embedding` BLOB side table
(migration `0027`), sync repo methods to read/write embedding rows, an idempotent
`foreman learnings backfill-embeddings` CLI command, and write-path embedding in
`worker-result-applier` so every learning add/update keeps its vector current. No
retrieval behavior changes in this ticket — the vectors are consumed by follow-up tickets.

## Context

Retrieval is the measured bottleneck of the learning system: FTS baseline recall@5 = 0.474
with 42% zero-recall cases (`src/eval/retrieval/README.md`). Exact-token FTS can't match
"typecheck" to "type-check". Embeddings fix that and are also the substrate for write-time
dedup. Decisions ratified 2026-07-09: local model (no external API in the serve loop) =
fastembed + bge-small-en-v1.5; BLOB side table + brute-force cosine (corpus ~113 learnings
→ ~170KB of vectors; sqlite-vec is deferred as premature below ~50K rows).

Two follow-up tickets depend on this one (hybrid search; write-time dedup), so keep the
port surface minimal and the repo methods synchronous.

## Out of scope

* Any change to `searchLearnings` / ranking / the retrieval bench (next ticket).
* Dedup, `duplicate_of`, provenance columns (ticket after that).
* sqlite-vec or any ANN indexing.
* API-based embedding providers (the port makes them possible later; don't build one).

## Instructions

### 1. Embedder port + fastembed provider

New `src/embeddings/embedder.ts` defining the port, mirroring the repo's port/impl layout
(`src/repos/learning-repo.ts` → `src/repos/impl/sqlite-learning-repo.ts`):

```ts
export interface Embedder {
  readonly modelId: string;   // e.g. "bge-small-en-v1.5"
  readonly dims: number;      // 384
  embed(texts: string[]): Promise<Float32Array[]>;
}
```

Impl at `src/embeddings/impl/fastembed-embedder.ts` using the `fastembed` npm package
(`EmbeddingModel.BGESmallENV15`). Lazy-init the underlying model on first `embed` call.
fastembed downloads the ONNX model (~30MB) to a local cache on first init — point its
cache dir at a stable location (workspace `.cache/` or the package default) and make sure
no network is needed after the first run. Commit `pnpm-lock.yaml` with the dependency.

### 2. Migration 0027 — side table

`migrations/0027_learning_embedding.sql` (0026 is taken; the runner sorts lexically by
full filename — `src/repos/impl/sqlite-migration-runner.ts:15` — so the duplicated
`0019_*` pair is harmless; just use max-prefix+1):

```sql
CREATE TABLE learning_embedding (
  learning_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES learning(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  model TEXT NOT NULL,
  dims INTEGER NOT NULL,
  vector BLOB NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ', 'now'))
);
```

Match the style of `migrations/0026_learning_search_event.sql`.

### 3. Sync repo methods

Extend `LearningRepo` (`src/repos/learning-repo.ts`) + `SqliteLearningRepo` with
synchronous methods (the embedder is async; the repo must stay sync — vectors are
computed above the repo and passed in):

* `upsertLearningEmbedding(input: { learningId, model, dims, vector: Float32Array })`
* `listLearningIdsMissingEmbedding(model: string): string[]` — no row OR model mismatch OR
  embedding `updated_at` older than the learning's `updated_at`
* `getLearningEmbeddings(filters?: { repos?: string[] }): { learningId, model, dims, vector: Float32Array }[]`

Serialize as the raw `Float32Array` buffer; round-trip it in a repo test.

### 4. Write-path embed in the applier

`src/orchestration/worker-result-applier.ts` has **two** `learningMutations` loops
(~line 224 and ~line 433). Extract one shared private helper that applies the mutations
and then embeds: text = `` `${title}\n${content}` `` (on update, re-embed only when title
or content changed). Inject the `Embedder` via the applier's `deps`.

Embedding failure must NOT fail the apply — the learning write itself is the priority.
Log a loud warning and leave the row for backfill (mirror the telemetry-never-fails
discipline documented at `src/cli.ts:126`).

### 5. Backfill CLI command

Add `foreman learnings backfill-embeddings <workspace>` next to the existing `learnings`
subcommands (`src/cli.ts:349`). Idempotent: embed exactly the ids from
`listLearningIdsMissingEmbedding(modelId)`, batch through `embed()`, upsert, then print
`{ total, embedded, skipped }`. Running it twice in a row must do zero work the second time.

### 6. Verify

`pnpm run check` + `pnpm test`. Unit tests use a fake `Embedder` (deterministic vectors —
never download the model in tests): applier embeds on add and on content-changing update,
skips on no-op update, survives an embedder throw with the learning still written; repo
BLOB round-trip preserves Float32Array values; backfill idempotency (second run embeds 0).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `learning_embedding` table exists via migration 0027; checksum-stable
- [ ] Learning add/update through the applier produces/refreshes an embedding row; embedder failure never fails the apply (test-covered)
- [ ] `foreman learnings backfill-embeddings` is idempotent (test-covered)
- [ ] `searchLearnings` behavior byte-identical to before (no retrieval change in this ticket)
- [ ] `pnpm run check` + `pnpm test` pass; `pnpm-lock.yaml` committed with the new dependency; no unrelated diff churn

title: Hybrid learnings retrieval: FTS bm25 + cosine fused with RRF, behind the existing search CLI, bench-gated
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Depends on tasks: ENG-5634
```

## Summary

Make `foreman learnings search` hybrid: embed the queries (async, in the CLI layer),
retrieve FTS bm25 and brute-force cosine candidate lists, fuse with reciprocal rank
fusion in a new sync repo method, and return the same output shape through the same CLI —
agents and prompts don't change. Extend the retrieval bench to score the hybrid pipeline;
the gate for this ticket is beating the committed FTS baseline (recall@5 0.474, MRR 0.546)
on the SAME pinned fixtures, and committing the new numbers.

## Context

The committed baseline (`src/eval/retrieval/README.md`) shows exact-token FTS misses
paraphrases entirely: 11 of 26 labeled cases retrieve nothing relevant in the top 10, and
recall@5 == recall@10 (FTS hits immediately or never). Cosine over the embeddings landed
by the dependency ticket is the recall lever; bm25 stays in the fusion because exact
token/id matches are precision gold when they do hit. RRF is the fusion choice: rank-based,
no score-scale calibration between bm25 (negative, unbounded) and cosine ([-1, 1]).

## Out of scope

* Push injection into worker prompts (M4 — gated on this ticket's bench numbers).
* Dedup / `duplicate_of` (parallel ticket owns the write path).
* Query expansion, LLM re-ranking, sqlite-vec.
* Prompt/template changes — the CLI contract is deliberately unchanged.
* Regenerating bench fixtures (hard rule in the bench README).

## Instructions

### 1. Sync hybrid method in the repo

`src/repos/impl/sqlite-learning-repo.ts`: add `searchLearningsHybrid(filters, queryVectors: Float32Array[])`
to `LearningRepo`. Per query: existing bm25 candidate list (reuse the `learning_fts` path
at ~line 145) + cosine ranking of the same in-scope corpus via `getLearningEmbeddings`
(brute force is fine: ~113 vectors, microseconds). Fuse per-query lists with RRF
(`score = Σ 1/(k + rank)`, k = 60), then merge across queries the way `searchLearnings`
already merges multi-query results. Return `LearningSearchRecord[]` with `score` = fused
score, honoring `limit`/`offset` after fusion. The repo stays synchronous — vectors come
in as arguments; no embedder inside the repo.

### 2. CLI: embed queries, degrade gracefully

`src/cli.ts` `learnings search` handler (~lines 349–390): embed the `--query` values with
the fastembed embedder from the dependency ticket, call `searchLearningsHybrid`. If the
embedder fails to init or `learning_embedding` has no rows for the scope, fall back to
plain `searchLearnings` with a stderr warning — a degraded search must never become a
failed search (same discipline as retrieval telemetry, `src/cli.ts:126`). Telemetry
recording is unchanged (`hitIds`/`hitScores` now carry fused results).

### 3. Extend the bench

`src/eval/retrieval/run.ts`: add `0027_learning_embedding.sql` to `BENCH_MIGRATIONS`; at
bench setup embed the pinned corpus with the real fastembed model (first run downloads it
once; embeddings are deterministic per model version). Score BOTH pipelines — FTS-only and
hybrid — and report them side by side in `formatRetrievalReport` (and `--json`) so the
delta is visible in one run.

### 4. Gate + commit the numbers

Hybrid must beat recall@5 0.474 on the pinned fixtures; also report MRR and the
zero-recall count (expected to drop well below 11). Update the committed numbers in
`src/eval/retrieval/README.md` and the baseline guard test from ENG-5632 to pin the new
hybrid numbers (keep the FTS numbers documented as the historical baseline). If hybrid
does NOT beat the baseline, do not merge a worse default — report the numbers on the
ticket instead.

### 5. Verify

`pnpm run check` + `pnpm test`. Unit tests for RRF fusion with a fake embedder
(deterministic vectors): paraphrase case that FTS misses but cosine catches, exact-token
case where bm25 leads, fallback path when no embeddings exist. Bench tests assert
structured metrics fields, not one narrative string. Run `foreman eval retrieval` and
paste both pipelines' metrics into the PR description.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `foreman learnings search` returns hybrid results through the unchanged CLI contract (flags, output shape, telemetry)
- [ ] Missing/failed embedder degrades to FTS-only with a warning, never a hard failure (test-covered)
- [ ] `foreman eval retrieval` reports FTS and hybrid side by side; hybrid recall@5 > 0.474 on the pinned fixtures
- [ ] New numbers committed in `src/eval/retrieval/README.md` + guard test updated; fixtures untouched
- [ ] `pnpm run check` + `pnpm test` pass; no unrelated diff churn
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Verification gate (post-merge)

- [ ] PR merged to `master`
- [ ] Repo `foreman`: **skip** CircleCI deployment verification (no deployment)

* 01KT0ZJE4X59QR63CBW27JTRNM: Assert structured payload fields in prompt-render tests, not just one narrative field

title: Write-time near-duplicate flagging (duplicate_of) + learning provenance (source_task_id)
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Depends on tasks: ENG-5634
```

## Summary

Migration `0028` adds two nullable columns to `learning`: `duplicate_of` (id of the
learning this one near-duplicates) and `source_task_id` (which task's attempt produced
it). The applier threads the task id into every learning add, and on add runs a
nearest-neighbor check against in-scope embeddings: above a calibrated cosine threshold,
the learning is still stored but flagged with `duplicate_of` — never silently dropped.
The learnings UI surfaces the flag. Threshold is calibrated against the committed corpus
fixture and pinned by a test.

## Context

The "search before add" policy is honor-system dedup and it demonstrably fails: 5
near-identical pairs exist in the live corpus (ENG-5444 labeling; e.g. "Run `yarn run check`, not `yarn check`" is stored twice under different titles). Duplicates dilute
search results. Decision D3 (ratified 2026-07-09): store + flag (observable, reversible)
rather than hard-reject or auto-merge; auto-convert-to-update can come later once the
threshold is trusted. Learnings also have no provenance — `source_task_id` makes the
capture→task link a column instead of a reconstruction from attempt windows (which is
what the retrieval bench had to do).

**Parallelism guard:** the hybrid-search ticket may run in parallel off the same
dependency. This ticket owns the WRITE path only — do not modify `searchLearnings`,
`searchLearningsHybrid`, the search CLI handler, or the retrieval bench.

## Out of scope

* Excluding flagged duplicates from retrieval (M5 lifecycle work — flag only, behavior unchanged).
* Auto-converting a duplicate add into an update (D3 phase 2).
* Merging the existing 5 live pairs (separate one-shot cleanup, handled outside this ticket).
* Any search/read-path or bench change.

## Instructions

### 1. Migration 0028

`migrations/0028_learning_duplicate_flag_provenance.sql` (0027 is claimed by the
dependency ticket; runner sorts lexically — max-prefix+1):

```sql
ALTER TABLE learning ADD COLUMN duplicate_of TEXT REFERENCES learning(id);
ALTER TABLE learning ADD COLUMN source_task_id TEXT;
```

Both nullable; no backfill (history stays null).

### 2. Repo: add-path inputs + NN lookup

`src/repos/learning-repo.ts` + `src/repos/impl/sqlite-learning-repo.ts`:

* `addLearning` input gains optional `sourceTaskId` and `duplicateOf`; persist both.
  Include the two fields in `LearningRecord` so `get`/`list` expose them.
* New sync method `nearestLearningEmbedding(vector: Float32Array, filters?: { repos?: string[] }): { learningId: string; similarity: number } | undefined`
  — brute-force cosine over `getLearningEmbeddings` scope, excluding nothing (a duplicate
  of a flagged duplicate still points at its nearest neighbor).

### 3. Applier: flag on add

In the shared learning-mutation helper from the dependency ticket
(`src/orchestration/worker-result-applier.ts`): on `add`, embed the new content first,
call `nearestLearningEmbedding` scoped to the mutation's repo + `shared`; if similarity ≥
threshold, set `duplicateOf` to the neighbor and log a loud info with both ids and the
similarity. Thread `input.task.id` into `sourceTaskId` on every add. Embedder failure
skips the dedup check (the add still lands, un-flagged) — never fail the apply.

### 4. Calibrate the threshold

Write the calibration as a committed test, not a one-off script: embed the pinned corpus
(`src/eval/retrieval/fixtures/corpus.json`) with a real-model fixture or the fastembed
model, compute pairwise cosine, and assert the known near-identical pairs score above the
chosen threshold while a sample of legitimate same-topic neighbors scores below. Start
from the ranked pairwise list: the true duplicate pairs should be cleanly separated from
the rest — pick the threshold in that gap (expect roughly 0.90–0.95 for bge-small; let
the data decide). Document the chosen value and the top-10 pairwise table in the PR
description. Put the threshold in one named constant next to the applier logic.

### 5. UI surfacing

`ui/src/pages/learnings/`: show a "duplicate" badge on flagged learnings and the id they
point at (link if the page supports it). Follow the UI rules: design tokens only, spacing
owned by the parent container, do not modify `components/ui` (vendored).

### 6. Verify

`pnpm run check` + `pnpm test`. Tests with a fake embedder: add above threshold → stored
WITH `duplicate_of` set (not dropped); below threshold → stored clean; embedder failure →
stored clean, no flag, apply succeeds; `source_task_id` populated on every applier add.
Repo test: columns round-trip through add/get/list.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration 0028 adds `duplicate_of` + `source_task_id`; both nullable, no backfill
- [ ] Applier adds always carry `source_task_id`; near-dup adds are stored + flagged, never dropped (test-covered)
- [ ] Threshold pinned by a calibration test over the committed corpus fixture; the 5 known pairs sit above it, sampled non-dup neighbors below
- [ ] Learnings UI shows the duplicate flag; no `components/ui` edits
- [ ] No changes to search methods, search CLI, or the retrieval bench
- [ ] `pnpm run check` + `pnpm test` pass; no unrelated diff churn
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Verification gate (post-merge)

- [ ] PR merged to `master`
- [ ] Repo `foreman`: **skip** CircleCI deployment verification (no deployment)

title: Surcharge config UI: full edit, supersession diff, name vocabulary, multi-service scoping
description:
> Foreman-ready Linear ticket. Follow-up iteration on the surcharge config UI (shipped in lynk-frontend PR #1136 "surcharge-config-ui", merged 2026-06-23).

## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: lynk-frontend
```

Items 1, 2 and 4 below are frontend-only and are the Foreman scope. **Item 3 (name vocabulary) is out of scope for this run** - it needs a `shipping-service` schema or lookup-table change and carries an unresolved design decision. Split it into its own ticket before working it.

## Context

Ryan and Ross used the UI live during the 2026-07-10 carrier surcharge alignment meeting and raised five pieces of feedback. Each was checked against the code before writing this ticket. Two are already fixed and are recorded here only so nobody re-does them. Three are real.

## Background: how the model actually works

* `Surcharge` rows are **immutable and append-only**. There is no `PUT`/`PATCH` endpoint - only `POST /v1/surcharges`, `GET /v1/surcharges`, `DELETE /v1/surcharges/{id}`.
* "Updating" a surcharge means inserting a new row with the same `(name, scope)` and a later `validFrom`.
* There is **no supersession concept in the schema** - no `supersedes` / `supersededBy` column. "Latest `validFrom` wins" is computed client-side in `computeSurchargeStatuses` (`surcharge-columns.tsx`).
* `name` is a plain `String` column, not an enum. `prisma/seedSurcharges.ts` establishes a de facto vocabulary ("Fuel Surcharge", "Security Surcharge", ...) by convention only.
* Scope is `carrierId` XOR `carrierServiceId` - two mutually exclusive nullable FK scalars, enforced by a DB CHECK constraint, `Surcharge.validateProps`, and a discriminated union on the DTO. One row can never target many services.
* Conditions are a typed, validated JSON tree (`applicability` column, `SurchargeApplicability` VO), deliberately independent of the Automation `Condition` machinery.

Relevant files, all under `src/pages/platform/carriers/carrier-details/`: `surcharges-card.tsx`, `surcharge-columns.tsx`, `create-surcharge-dialog-content.tsx`, `update-surcharge-rate-dialog-content.tsx`, `surcharge-conditions-builder.tsx`, `surcharge-applicability.ts`, `use-carrier-surcharges.ts`.

## In scope

### 1. Editing an existing surcharge is not possible (only the rate)

The "Update rate" dialog exposes **only** `rate` and `validFrom`. Name, applies-to, charge method and conditions render read-only. There is no path in the UI to change an existing surcharge's conditions at all.

This is what Ryan hit when he said condition editing "doesn't reflect the change" - he was looking at a read-only summary and expecting an editable field.

Scaffolding already exists: `toConditionsForm()` in `surcharge-applicability.ts` is documented as unused and "ready for an 'edit conditions' follow-up". Wire it up.

Because rows are immutable, "edit" here means "prefill a create form from the existing row and post a new row with a later `validFrom`". **Decision needed before implementation:** is that the right UX, or do we want real mutability?

### 2. Supersession chain and before/after diff are invisible

The table does show superseded rows with an Active / Scheduled / Superseded status badge (see "Already fixed" below). What's missing:

* No explicit "supersedes X" link or chain visualisation.
* The update dialog prefills the new rate from the old value but shows no side-by-side old to new confirmation. You commit a rate change without seeing what it replaced.

Frontend-only. No schema or API change required.

### 4. "Applies to" should be multi-select

Today: single-select, backed by the `carrierId` XOR `carrierServiceId` model.

True multi-service scoping (one row targeting many services) needs a schema change and would break the CHECK constraint and the DTO union. **Cheaper option, and the one to build:** keep the model, deliver the multi-select purely as a frontend affordance that fires N sequential `POST /v1/surcharges` (one row per selected service). Ops gets the ergonomics; the model stays as-is. Joe's example from the meeting - "have a condition for domestic vs international, or one record whose condition is that it's a domestic shipment" - suggests conditions may be the better lever than multi-select anyway.

## Out of scope for the Foreman run

### 3. Surcharge name is free text; should be a picker

Ops types the name by hand on every create. A typo silently creates a new `(name, scope)` group rather than superseding the intended one - which is a correctness bug, not just a papercut, given supersession keys on `name`.

Two options, needs a decision, and both touch `shipping-service`:

* Migrate `name` to an enum. Strongest guarantee, breaks on every new surcharge type.
* Curated lookup table feeding a frontend combobox with free-text fallback. Seed from `seedSurcharges.ts`.

## Already fixed - do not re-do

* **"You can't see superseded rows in this view."** You can. The table shows all rows for a carrier with an Active / Scheduled / Superseded badge, grouped by `name+scope`, sorted `name asc, validFrom desc`. The disagreement in the meeting ("you can't see them" vs "yeah you can") resolves in favour of the latter. The remaining gap is the chain/diff, captured as item 2 above.
* **"No tabular view across all surcharges; you navigate per-carrier/per-service."** Within a carrier there is now one unified table combining carrier-wide and all per-service rows, rendered once on `CarrierDetails`. What genuinely doesn't exist is a **global cross-carrier** view. `GetSurchargesInput.carrierId` is already nullable and the backend use-case supports returning everything - the frontend just never calls it that way. If a global table is wanted, it's frontend-only. Split into its own ticket if so.

## Correcting a claim made in the meeting

Condition editing was said to be **blocked on the Automation UI overhaul**, to be unblocked by reusing that refactor's condition builder. **This does not hold.**

`surcharge-conditions-builder.tsx` mirrors the Automation rule-card condition row but does not import it - it was rebuilt on shadcn/RHF rather than the legacy Ant builder. The backend condition shape is deliberately decoupled from Workflow machinery. The in-flight `workflow-editor/condition-panel` module is page-local, not a shared package, so there is nothing to reuse without extracting it first.

**Recommendation: do not gate this work on the Automation refactor.** It can proceed independently.

## Adjacent - flag before touching delete/edit semantics

`DeleteSurcharge` hard-deletes any row by id, including an **active** one. This was raised in PR #594 review and left open. If this ticket touches edit or delete semantics, resolve it: delete should probably be restricted to future-dated (Scheduled) rows.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] An existing surcharge's conditions can be edited from the UI, producing a new row with a later `validFrom`.
- [ ] The update flow shows old to new for the changed fields before commit.
- [ ] The table exposes the supersession relationship, not just the per-row status badge.
- [ ] "Applies to" accepts multiple services in one create action.
- [ ] Existing tests pass; new behaviour is covered.
- [ ] Post-merge: `Deploy prod` stage succeeds on CircleCI. If `Trigger E2E` runs, the E2E run passes after reconciliation against pre-existing failures.

## Source

2026-07-10 carrier surcharge alignment meeting (Joe, Ryan, Ross, Gerd).

title: Formalize the retrieval bench harness over the committed fixtures
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Branch: learning-retrieval-bench-fixtures
```

## Summary

Formalize the retrieval bench prototype already on this branch (`src/eval/retrieval/`) into a first-class, tested harness with a CLI entry point. The fixtures (pinned corpus + 54 dual-labeled cases) and the committed FTS baseline (recall@5 0.474 / MRR 0.546 / 11 zero-recall cases) are DONE and must not be regenerated — this ticket is about the harness code around them.

## Context

m0-2 of the learning-system arc: retrieval improvements (hybrid embedding search, prompt injection) need an acceptance gate. The branch `learning-retrieval-bench-fixtures` already carries `src/eval/retrieval/{README.md,baseline.mjs,fixtures/corpus.json,fixtures/cases.json}` — read the README first; it documents fixture provenance, the self-provenance exclusion rule, and the committed baseline numbers. Unlike the prompt evals (`src/eval/registry.ts`), this bench is deterministic and model-free, so it does NOT join `EVAL_REGISTRY` — it is a sibling harness.

## Out of scope

* Regenerating or editing `fixtures/*.json` (fixture-pinned baseline; changing fixtures invalidates the committed numbers).
* Any change to `searchLearnings` or the FTS pipeline.
* Embedding/hybrid retrieval (next milestone; it will consume this bench).
* Wiring into `EVAL_REGISTRY` / `EvalDefinition` (fixture union doesn't fit a model-free bench).

## Instructions

### 1. Port `baseline.mjs` to TypeScript inside the harness

Create `src/eval/retrieval/run.ts` (+ small `score.ts` if it helps): seed an in-memory better-sqlite3 DB from `fixtures/corpus.json` using migrations `0004_memory_tables.sql` + `0005_learning_fts.sql`, instantiate the real `SqliteLearningRepo`, replay each case's `queries` with repo scope `["shared", ...case.repos]`, score recall@5/recall@10/MRR against `expected`, plus zero-recall-case count. Keep the scoring semantics of `baseline.mjs` EXACTLY (verify by matching its numbers before deleting it — delete `baseline.mjs` in the same PR once parity is shown).

### 2. CLI entry

Add a `retrieval` branch to the existing `eval` command in `src/cli.ts` (or a sibling subcommand) — `foreman eval retrieval [--json]`. It must not require a workspace or a live runner. `--json` emits the metrics object; default output is the metrics + a per-case table.

### 3. Guard the baseline

Add a test (`src/eval/retrieval/__tests__/retrieval-bench.test.ts`) that runs the harness against the committed fixtures and asserts the exact baseline metrics (recall@5 0.474, recall@10 0.474, MRR 0.546, zeroRecallCases 11, labeledCases 26). This makes any accidental fixture or scoring drift loud. Also unit-test the scorer on tiny synthetic inputs (hit at rank 1, hit at rank 6, no hit, multi-expected partial).

### 4. Verify

`yarn run check` (not `yarn check`) + `yarn test`. Confirm `foreman eval retrieval --json` reproduces the README numbers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `foreman eval retrieval` runs offline/deterministically and reproduces the committed baseline numbers
- [ ] Baseline-guard test asserts the exact metrics; scorer unit tests cover rank edge cases
- [ ] `baseline.mjs` removed after parity is demonstrated; README updated to point at the CLI

title: Record learning search/get telemetry in the workspace DB
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
```

## Summary

Add a `learning_search_event` table + repo and record an event from every `foreman learnings search` and `foreman learnings get` CLI invocation (timestamp, queries/ids, repo scopes, hit ids + scores, zero-hit flag, optional caller stage). Add an optional `--caller <stage>` flag to both commands and thread it through the documented CLI invocations in the prompt templates. No retrieval behavior changes.

## Context

Live data shows 81% of the workspace's 113 learnings have never been read (44 read events across 405 execution attempts), but `read_count`/`applied_count` are lifetime aggregates — we cannot see what was searched, what matched, and what came back empty. Later retrieval improvements (hybrid embedding search, prompt injection) need this telemetry to prove themselves against live behavior. First increment of the learning-system improvement arc.

## Out of scope

* Any change to `searchLearnings` ranking or the FTS pipeline.
* Logging the `GET /api/learnings` HTTP path — that is the human UI, not agent retrieval; recording it would pollute the agent-usage signal.
* UI/dashboard work.

## Instructions

### 1. Migration

Add `migrations/00XX_learning_search_event.sql` (next free number — note `0019` is duplicated in the dir; check how `SqliteMigrationRunner` orders before picking). Table columns: `id` (pk), `created_at`, `workspace` not needed (DB is per-workspace), `kind` (`search` | `get`), `caller` (nullable TEXT), `queries` (JSON array, full text), `repos` (JSON array), `requested_ids` (JSON array, for `get`), `hit_ids` (JSON array), `hit_scores` (JSON array, aligned with `hit_ids`), `zero_hit` (INTEGER 0/1). Follow the CHECK-constraint style of `migrations/0004_memory_tables.sql`.

### 2. Repo port + impl

New `src/repos/learning-search-event-repo.ts` (interface: `recordEvent(...)` + a `listEvents(filters)` read method) and `src/repos/impl/sqlite-learning-search-event-repo.ts`, mirroring the port/impl split of `src/repos/learning-repo.ts` / `impl/sqlite-learning-repo.ts`. Wire into `ForemanRepos` (`src/repos/foreman-repos.ts`) and `createRepos` (`src/repos/create-repos.ts`).

### 3. CLI recording + `--caller`

In `src/cli.ts`: the `learnings search` action (~line 336) and `learnings get` action (~line 364) gain an optional `--caller <stage>` option and record an event alongside the existing `incrementReadCount: true` reads. Telemetry insert must never fail the query — wrap it so a telemetry error logs and the result still returns.

### 4. Thread `--caller` through prompt templates

Update the documented invocations: `prompts/templates/plan.md` (Learnings CLI section, ~line 18) → `--caller plan`; `prompts/fragments/learning-policy.md` (step 2, line 6) → `--caller <action>` phrased per the fragment's action-tag vocabulary. Keep the flag optional — in-flight prompts without it must keep working (nullable column).

title: Inject a learnings index into the workspace plan prompt
description:
## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Depends on tasks: ENG-5622
```

## Summary

Render a compact learnings index (id, title, repo, confidence, tags) into `plan.md` at workspace-plan render time via a new `{{context:learnings-toc}}` token; rewrite the plan template's Learnings CLI section to shortlist from the index and `foreman learnings get --id` for bodies (search stays as fallback); strip the index section when plan.md is embedded into worker prompts.

## Context

Planners must currently guess FTS query terms to discover learnings and mostly don't — 81% of the corpus has never been read. At ~113 learnings the full index is ~2–3K tokens: cheaper and more reliable than keyword guessing for the planning stage. Explicitly a bridge until embedding-based retrieval lands; per-plan-render telemetry (previous ticket) tells us when it stops scaling. Depends on the telemetry ticket because both edit the same `prompts/templates/plan.md` section and this rewrite must preserve its documented `--caller plan` invocations.

## Out of scope

* Any direct learnings injection into execution/review/retry prompts (a later, relevance-filtered increment).
* Any search-pipeline or ranking change.
* Embeddings.

## Instructions

### 1. Fetch + render the index

`src/planning/render-workspace-plan.ts` (~line 18): fetch the corpus via `foremanRepos.learnings.listLearnings()` — the no-search path deliberately does NOT increment `read_count` (an index render is not an agent read). Both live callers (`src/cli.ts:245`, `src/cli.ts:325`) already pass `foremanRepos`; make the param required rather than degrading. Pass the listing into `renderPlanPrompt` (`src/planning/render-plan-prompt.ts`) and render as a markdown table (use `markdownSection` from `src/prompts/template-renderer.ts:44` — a table beats `jsonSection` on tokens) into a new `{{context:learnings-toc}}` token. Columns: id, title, repo, confidence, tags.

### 2. Delimit for stripping

Wrap the rendered section in stable markers `<!-- learnings-index:start -->` / `<!-- learnings-index:end -->`.

### 3. Strip from worker embeds

Worker prompts embed plan.md verbatim via the file-local `renderWorkspacePlan` in `src/execution/render-worker-prompt.ts` (~line 241, consumed at ~line 328 as `{{context:workspace-plan}}`). Strip everything between the markers there so execution attempts do not inherit the ~2–3K-token index.

### 4. Truncation guardrail

Above ~300 entries, truncate by confidence-then-recency and state in the rendered section how many entries were omitted — no silent caps.

### 5. Rewrite the template section

`prompts/templates/plan.md` (Learnings CLI section, ~lines 15–22): instruct shortlist-from-index → `foreman learnings get automation-pilot --id <id> --caller plan` for bodies; keep `learnings search --caller plan` documented as fallback for topics not evident in titles. Preserve the `Relevant Learnings` task-section requirements.

### 6. Verify

`yarn run check` + `yarn test`. Add prompt-render tests (sibling to `src/__tests__/prompts.test.ts`): index present with correct rows, truncation note above cap, empty-corpus rendering, and worker-prompt embed contains NO index between markers. Assert structured fields, not just one narrative string.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Rendered `plan.md` contains a "Workspace Learnings Index" section (id/title/repo/confidence/tags), with zero `read_count` side effects
- [ ] Worker prompts receive plan.md with the index section stripped (covered by render test)
- [ ] Truncation above the cap is stated in the rendered section, never silent
- [ ] `yarn run check` + `yarn test` pass; no unrelated diff churn

title: graders.ts parseJudgeBlock drops a valid verdict when the payload embeds the literal `<judge>` tag (same class as ENG-5450)
description:
## Summary

`parseJudgeBlock` in `src/eval/graders.ts` extracts a `<judge>…</judge>` block via `lastIndexOf("<judge>")` / `lastIndexOf("</judge>")`. This is the same brittle backward scan ENG-5450 fixed in `parseWorkerResult`: if the judge payload JSON itself embeds the literal `<judge>` (or `</judge>`) string, the scan latches onto the in-payload mention, slices a truncated fragment, and returns `null` (verdict dropped) even though a well-formed block is present.

It was deliberately left out of ENG-5450's scope (separate helper, not the shared one) and flagged in PR #109 review by both the reviewer and the author as worth a follow-up ticket. Lower-likelihood than the worker-result case because judge verdicts rarely quote the `<judge>` delimiter, but the same latent correctness bug.

## Fix

Apply the ENG-5450 pattern (`src/execution/worker-result.ts`): anchor on the last close tag and try candidate open tags outermost-first, accepting the first slice that parses; precompute offsets and cap parse attempts so the scan stays linear, since judge stdout is also model output that can echo the tag.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Regression test: a judge payload whose JSON embeds the literal `<judge>` / `</judge>` string parses to the correct verdict via `parseJudgeVerdict` and `parseSummaryJudgeVerdict`
- [ ] Existing grader tests still pass
- [ ] Typecheck passes
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

## Context

Origin: ENG-5450 (`parseWorkerResult` tag-in-payload fix), PR [https://github.com/mebens-invenco/foreman/pull/109](<https://github.com/mebens-invenco/foreman/pull/109>) — see the review thread noting the same latent bug in `parseJudgeBlock`.

title: Foreman issue manager — API: enable/disable toggle + agentEnabled/frontmatter fields
description:
> Foreman-ready Linear ticket. Label `agent:tars`, assigned to me, state Todo.

## Foreman metadata

```
Agent:
  Repos: foreman
  Depends on tasks: ENG-5384
```

## Summary

Expose the data + action the manager UI needs:

1. `POST /api/tasks/:taskId/agent-enabled` — toggles the `agent:disabled` exclude label (wrapping the existing `LinearTaskSystem.updateLabels`).
2. Two derived fields on `GET /api/tasks`: `agentEnabled` (no exclude label present) and `frontmatter` ({state, repos, detail}) computed by reusing Foreman's own `parseLinearMetadata`.

Builds on [ENG-5384](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5384/foreman-issue-manager-backend-excludelabels-config-scout-skip)'s `excludeLabels` config.

## Out of scope

* The manager UI — follow-up UI ticket.
* At-risk detection — separate enhancement ticket.
* In-UI editing of the description/frontmatter (read-only flag in v1).

## Instructions

### 1\. Toggle endpoint

`src/http.ts`, near the other mutating routes (`POST /api/scheduler/pause` \~line 1002):

```
POST /api/tasks/:taskId/agent-enabled   body: { enabled: boolean }
```

* `enabled=false` → `taskSystem.updateLabels({ taskId, add: excludeLabels, remove: [] })`
* `enabled=true`  → `taskSystem.updateLabels({ taskId, add: [], remove: excludeLabels })`

`updateLabels({ taskId, add, remove })` already exists (`src/tasking/impl/linear-task-system.ts:991`). Use the configured `excludeLabels` (add the first / remove all). Return the updated `agentEnabled`. 4xx if `excludeLabels` is empty (feature not enabled) or task not found — do not silently no-op.

### 2\. `agentEnabled` + `frontmatter` on `GET /api/tasks`

`GET /api/tasks` handler (`src/http.ts:557`). For each task:

```
agentEnabled = !task.labels.some((l) => excludeLabels.includes(l))
```

Frontmatter — **reuse the parser, parse the fetched description** (not the stale mirror `targets`):

```
const { targets } = parseLinearMetadata(description, branchName) // linear-task-system.ts:130
const blockPresent = /(^|\n)Agent:\s*\n((?:\s{2,}.+\n?)*)/i.test(description)
frontmatter =
  targets.length > 0 ? { state: "valid",  repos: targets.map(t => t.repoKey), detail: null }
  : blockPresent     ? { state: "broken", repos: [], detail: "Agent: block found, but no usable Repos:" }
  :                    { state: "missing", repos: [], detail: "No Agent: metadata block" }
```

Export `parseLinearMetadata` if not already; do **not** re-implement the block detection — the parser is lenient (indent ≥2, `Repos:`|`Repo:`) and must stay the single source of truth. (Untagged issues aren't fetched here, so they simply won't appear with a frontmatter verdict — the UI renders `n/a` for those.)

### 3\. Tests

* Toggle route: `enabled:false` adds the label, `enabled:true` removes it (assert the `updateLabels` call args); 4xx on empty `excludeLabels` / unknown task.
* `/api/tasks`: `agentEnabled` reflects exclude-label presence; `frontmatter` returns valid/broken/missing for fixtures (valid block, block-without-Repos, no block). Same fixtures should match `parseLinearMetadata`'s own behavior.

### 4\. Verify

```
yarn run check
yarn test
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/tasks/:id/agent-enabled` toggles the exclude label via `updateLabels`
- [ ] Endpoint 4xxs (not silently) when `excludeLabels` is unset or task missing
- [ ] `GET /api/tasks` returns `agentEnabled` and `frontmatter:{state,repos,detail}`
- [ ] `frontmatter` reuses `parseLinearMetadata` on the fetched description (no re-implementation)
- [ ] Tests cover toggle add/remove + all three frontmatter states
- [ ] `yarn run check` + `yarn test` pass
- [ ] No unrelated diff churn
- [ ] PR opened against `master`

- [ ] CircleCI deployment verified for merge commit:
  * Repo `foreman`: **skip** (no deployment)

* 01KT1236TBD689A21C8J41ANE6: Run `yarn run check`, not `yarn check`, to typecheck foreman

title: Reclassify Direct Freight (DFE) PER_PACKAGE → PER_CONSIGNMENT (prerequisite for per-parcel cache)
description:
## ⚠️ Update 2026-06-05 — re-scoped + runnable prod script

**Scope change.** [ENG-5343](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5343/per-parcel-multi-package-carrier-rate-caching-auspost-seko) (per-parcel multi-package caching) has been **dropped** — draft PR closed, branch deleted. Decision: keep only **per-consignment** caching; **never cache per-package** (≈98% of shipments are single-package; the gateway doesn't return per-parcel prices for multi-package; prod read-access to verify composition is blocked).

**What this means for this ticket.** With per-package excluded from caching, DFE's `PER_PACKAGE` flag is **no longer a live-mispricing exposure** — a `PER_PACKAGE` method simply falls through to a live bundled call, which prices DFE correctly. So this reclassification is now a **config-correctness + caching-enablement cleanup, not a safety blocker**, and it no longer blocks anything ([ENG-5343](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5343/per-parcel-multi-package-carrier-rate-caching-auspost-seko) is closed). Still worth doing: it makes the flag match DFE's verified per-consignment API behaviour and lets DFE benefit from the (safe) per-consignment cache. *(Priority could reasonably drop High → Medium.)*

**Only gateway carriers are in scope.** No-gateway carriers (NZ Post, Go People, USPS, DPD, Evri, …) store **hardcoded rates in** `carrier_rates` **and are always served from there** — there is no live API to deviate from, so their flag can't be *detected* as wrong and must be **left alone**. DFE is the only gateway carrier with an empirically-proven-wrong flag. (This also explains the earlier "Go People has multi-package cache rows written today" observation — that's the intended hardcoded-rate path, not a bug.)

### Precise production scope (live re-pull 2026-06-05 · ClickHouse `shipping.shipping_methods` FINAL, is_deleted=0)

DFE = one carrier service `ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj` ("Standard Parcel"), two DFE rate accounts:

| DFE rate account | `rates_carrier_account_id` | PER_CONSIGNMENT | PER_PACKAGE |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Prestons | `o12ajl4pvmubpybfdczlw6ha` | — | **203** |
| Epping | `yjrafz731090vyd771gy5cca` | 45 | **49** |

**252 methods flip** `PER_PACKAGE → PER_CONSIGNMENT` (203 Prestons + 49 Epping). The 45 Epping rows are already correct. End state = **297, all** `PER_CONSIGNMENT`. The same Epping rate account holding *both* flags confirms this is config drift. All rows are `STATE_STATE`, AU domestic.

### Cache — no purge needed

Checked `shipping.carrier_rates` (the cache; 84,545 rows, and it is live — TGE has 42k rows written today). **DFE's carrier service has 0 cached rows** — expected, because `PER_PACKAGE` is excluded from gateway caching, so DFE was never written. After the flip, DFE becomes cache-eligible and populates **fresh** from live per-consignment calls. Nothing stale to invalidate.

---

### ▶ The fix — NEON / Postgres (production write DB)

> ⚠️ Run on **prod Neon Postgres**. ClickHouse is a **read-only analytics mirror** — do **not** run the fix there. Table is case-sensitive (`"ShippingMethod"`); column is snake_case (`rate_calculation_method`).

```sql
BEGIN;

-- (optional) capture affected ids first, for a precise rollback later
--   SELECT id FROM "ShippingMethod"
--   WHERE carrier_service_id = 'ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj'
--     AND rate_calculation_method = 'PER_PACKAGE';

-- Preflight — expect PER_CONSIGNMENT=45, PER_PACKAGE=252
SELECT rate_calculation_method, count(*) AS methods
FROM "ShippingMethod"
WHERE carrier_service_id = 'ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj'
GROUP BY 1;

-- Fix
UPDATE "ShippingMethod"
SET rate_calculation_method = 'PER_CONSIGNMENT',
    updated_at = now()
WHERE carrier_service_id = 'ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj'
  AND rate_calculation_method = 'PER_PACKAGE';
-- expect: UPDATE 252

-- Postflight — expect a single row: PER_CONSIGNMENT=297
SELECT rate_calculation_method, count(*) AS methods
FROM "ShippingMethod"
WHERE carrier_service_id = 'ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj'
GROUP BY 1;

COMMIT;   -- or ROLLBACK; if the counts don't match
```

**Notes for the runner**

* Scoping by `carrier_service_id` alone is exact — DFE owns exactly this one service id, so it cannot touch any other carrier. The `count` checks are the safety gate; if preflight ≠ 45/252, **stop**.
* Raw `UPDATE` **bypasses the domain event** (`ShippingMethodUpdated` → EventBridge). The service reads `rateCalculationMethod` from Postgres at quote time, so its own behaviour corrects immediately; confirm nothing external depends on that event before running.
* Rollback after commit isn't a blind inverse — the originally-correct 45 rows are now indistinguishable from the flipped 252. Use the captured id list to revert precisely.

### ✔ Verification — ClickHouse (analytics mirror, read-only; allow CDC lag)

```sql
SELECT rates_carrier_account_name, rate_calculation_method, count() AS methods
FROM shipping.shipping_methods FINAL
WHERE is_deleted = 0 AND carrier_service_id = 'ho6p3x7nmt4yvoocxz7rjjcj'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 2;
-- after deploy: every row PER_CONSIGNMENT (Prestons 203, Epping 94)
```

### Seed data

No seed change required. `seedShippingMethod` hardcodes `PER_CONSIGNMENT` (`idempotent-seed.ts:211`) and ignores `options.rateCalculationMethod`, so dev/staging already model DFE as `PER_CONSIGNMENT` — there is no wrong seeded value. (The hardcode swallowing the option is a latent helper-hygiene bug, optional to fix; it changes no values.)

---

*Original ticket (created 2026-06-04) below — evidence still valid; the "blocks* [ENG-5343](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5343/per-parcel-multi-package-carrier-rate-caching-auspost-seko) */ prerequisite" framing is superseded by the re-scope above.*

## Summary

Direct Freight Express (DFE) is configured `rateCalculationMethod = PER_PACKAGE` in production, but its rates API is **per-consignment** — it returns a single `TotalFreightCharge` for the whole consignment computed on the combined weight. **The flag is wrong.**

**Latent today — not an active incident.** Per-parcel cache composition is currently blocked, so DFE falls through to a live bundled call and prices correctly. (Originally framed as: the moment per-parcel caching is unblocked via [ENG-5343](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5343/per-parcel-multi-package-carrier-rate-caching-auspost-seko) the cache would compose `Σ(per-parcel)` for DFE — billing the whole-consignment rate per parcel, a 2–3× overprice across 252 methods / 219 accounts. [ENG-5343](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5343/per-parcel-multi-package-carrier-rate-caching-auspost-seko) has since been dropped, so this exposure no longer applies — see the re-scope above.)

## Evidence (verified live, 2026-06-04)

Probed DFE's live API (`webservices.directfreight.com.au`): a 2 kg / 3 kg / 10 kg consignment **all priced $15.26** — the combined-weight consignment rate, not per-parcel. `Σ(per-parcel singles)` would bill $15.26 **per parcel** → 2–3× overprice.

## Production scope (original snapshot 2026-06-04)

| Carrier | PER_PACKAGE methods | Live gateway? | Action |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| **Direct Freight Express** | **252** (1 service / 219 accounts) | **Yes** — per-consignment API | **Reclassify →** `PER_CONSIGNMENT` |
| NZ Post | 258 | No (static cards) | inert — leave |
| Go People | 4 | No (static cards) | inert — leave |

Only DFE has a live gateway, so it is the only `PER_PACKAGE` flag that can drive cache `Σ`-composition. NZ Post & Go People price from static `ShippingRate` cards keyed on total consignment weight regardless of the flag → harmless.

## References

* Notion design doc §9 "Cross-carrier verification → Config can't be trusted" (linked).
* Local analysis: `MQE_cross_carrier_verification.md`.

title: Build TGE cache activation primitive
description:
**Canceled 2026-05-14.** Per Ashish (2026-05-13 pm Slack): "Don't need another flag, no, and we shouldn't use `useOnlyCachedCarrierRates`. That flag is irrelevant for us in this project."

Verified in code (`CarrierRateService.ts`):

* **Cache READ path** quantizes billable weight against `shippingMethod.getCarrierWeightBrackets()`. If no brackets exist, quantization fails, that weight contributes nothing to the lookup query, and the cache returns empty for that carrier service. Execution falls through to `getCarrierRatesFromCarrierGateway` automatically.
* **Cache WRITE path** (line \~60+) has the same skip-on-no-brackets behaviour, gated by the existing `CARRIER_RATE_CACHE_WRITE` flag (already on globally).

So **the bracket-seeding ticket** [ENG-5137](https://linear.app/invenco/issue/ENG-5137/seed-tge-carrierweightbracket) **IS the activation mechanism** for TGE - no separate `production_usable` column, no new flag, no read-side gate. Adding brackets turns cache writes and reads on for the carrier; removing them reverts to gateway-only.

The "trust-then-flip" intent the M1 doc described becomes a **runbook step**, not a code feature: seed brackets in stage, watch the cache populate, sample-validate; if it looks right, repeat the seed in prod. Reversal = drop the brackets.

---

(Original description preserved below for audit trail.)

# Build TGE cache activation primitive

Add the trust-then-flip primitive to `CarrierRate`:

1. Schema: add `production_usable BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` column to `CarrierRate` (Prisma migration).
2. Flag: add `CARRIER_RATE_CACHE_PRODUCTION_USABLE` to `FeatureFlagName` enum.
3. Read gate: in the cache read path of `CarrierRateService`, only treat a cached row as a hit when `row.production_usable && CARRIER_RATE_CACHE_PRODUCTION_USABLE`.
